input.initialPrompt:
I'm setting up .NET MAUI development on Windows 11 from scratch. I need
to target both Android and Windows. I can't run commands right now — give
me a complete step-by-step plan with all the PowerShell commands I'll need,
including how to discover the right versions dynamically.

data.content:
Here's a complete setup plan for .NET MAUI on Windows 11 (Android + Windows targets):

---

## 1. Install .NET SDK

```powershell
# Check if .NET is already installed
dotnet --list-sdks

# Install via winget (discovers latest stable .NET 8/9 SDK)
winget search Microsoft.DotNet.SDK
winget install Microsoft.DotNet.SDK.9

# Verify
dotnet --version
```

## 2. Install the MAUI Workload

```powershell
# List available workloads to find the exact name
dotnet workload search maui

# Install MAUI (pulls in Android, Windows SDK dependencies)
dotnet workload install maui

# Verify installed workloads
dotnet workload list
```

## 3. Install Visual Studio Build Tools (Windows target needs WinUI/UWP support)

```powershell
winget install Microsoft.VisualStudio.2022.Community

# Or if you only want the build tools:
winget install Microsoft.VisualStudio.2022.BuildTools
```

Then add the required workloads via the VS Installer CLI:

```powershell
# Discover where vs_installer lives
$vsInstaller = "${env:ProgramFiles(x86)}\Microsoft Visual Studio\Installer\vs_installer.exe"

# Add .NET MAUI and UWP/WinUI workloads
& "$vsInstaller" modify --installPath "C:\Program Files\Microsoft Visual Studio\2022\Community" `
  --add Microsoft.VisualStudio.Workload.NetCrossPlat `
  --add Microsoft.VisualStudio.Workload.ManagedDesktop `
  --passive --norestart
```

> **Tip:** If you're unsure of the install path, discover it:
> ```powershell
> & "${env:ProgramFiles(x86)}\Microsoft Visual Studio\Installer\vswhere.exe" -latest -property installationPath
> ```

## 4. Android SDK Setup

The MAUI workload installs a basic Android SDK, but you may need to manage it:

```powershell
# Find where MAUI installed the Android SDK
$androidSdk = "$env:LOCALAPPDATA\Android\Sdk"
if (!(Test-Path $androidSdk)) {
    # Fallback: check dotnet's android SDK location
    $androidSdk = (dotnet android sdk info 2>$null | Select-String 'SDK Path').ToString().Split(':',2)[1].Trim()
}
Write-Host "Android SDK at: $androidSdk"

# Set ANDROID_HOME if not already set
[System.Environment]::SetEnvironmentVariable("ANDROID_HOME", $androidSdk, "User")
$env:ANDROID_HOME = $androidSdk

# Use sdkmanager to discover and install components
$sdkmanager = "$androidSdk\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat"

# List available platforms and pick the latest
& $sdkmanager --list | Select-String "platforms;android-"

# Install required components (adjust API level as needed)
& $sdkmanager "platforms;android-35" "build-tools;35.0.0" "platform-tools"

# Accept all licenses
& $sdkmanager --licenses   # answer 'y' to all
```

## 5. Android Emulator (optional but recommended)

```powershell
# Install emulator + a system image
& $sdkmanager "emulator" "system-images;android-35;google_apis;x86_64"

# Create an AVD
$avdmanager = "$androidSdk\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\avdmanager.bat"
& $avdmanager create avd -n "Pixel_MAUI" -k "system-images;android-35;google_apis;x86_64" --device "pixel_6"

# Verify Hyper-V / WHPX is enabled (required for emulator acceleration on Win 11)
Get-WindowsOptionalFeature -Online -FeatureName Microsoft-Hyper-V-All | Select State
# If disabled:
Enable-WindowsOptionalFeature -Online -FeatureName Microsoft-Hyper-V-All -NoRestart
```

## 6. Enable Windows Developer Mode (required for WinUI/sideloading)

```powershell
# Enable developer mode via registry
reg add "HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\AppModelUnlock" `
  /t REG_DWORD /f /v "AllowDevelopment" /d 1
```

## 7. Create & Run a Test Project

```powershell
# Scaffold a new MAUI app
dotnet new maui -n MauiTestApp
cd MauiTestApp

# Build for Windows (WinUI)
dotnet build -f net9.0-windows10.0.19041.0

# Build for Android
dotnet build -f net9.0-android

# Run on Windows
dotnet run -f net9.0-windows10.0.19041.0

# Run on Android emulator (start emulator first)
& "$env:ANDROID_HOME\emulator\emulator.exe" -avd Pixel_MAUI &
dotnet run -f net9.0-android
```

## 8. Troubleshooting Commands

```powershell
# Check all MAUI dependencies at once
dotnet workload repair

# See exact TFMs your SDK supports
dotnet --list-runtimes

# Verify Android SDK components
& $sdkmanager --list_installed

# Nuke and reinstall workloads if things go wrong
dotnet workload uninstall maui
dotnet workload install maui
```

---

**Key order of operations:** .NET SDK → MAUI workload → Visual Studio (for WinUI build support) → Android SDK/emulator → Developer Mode → verify with a test project. Adjust `net9.0` to `net8.0` if you prefer LTS.
